estimand_functional: Ψ_{λ,n}(O_1,…,O_n; η̂_λ) = τ̂_λ (clipped AIPW), with target rate ρ_n and matching minimax converse

symbols.name: rho_n
symbols.def: rho_n = inf over λ of [ clipped-tail bias(λ) + standard-deviation(λ) ]

assumptions.condition: 1 − e(X) ≥ η almost surely, for fixed η > 0
c₋·tᵏ ≤ P(e(X) ≤ t) ≤ c₊·tᵏ for all 0 < t ≤ t0
mu1(x) = g1(e(x)) on {x : e(x) ≤ t0}, with g1 ∈ Hᵝ(L_β)
|b_lambda(x) − mu1(x)| ≤ C_β·λᵝ for a.e. x with e(x) ≤ λ, all λ ∈ (0, t0]
Cross-fit nuisance estimates trained on independent folds satisfy E[ product-drift integrand | 𝒯 ] ≤ Δ_n(λ)
E[ phi_lambda² · 1{|phi_lambda| ≥ ε·√(n·I_lambda)} | 𝒯 ] ≤ C·I_lambda for all ε > 0
E[ (phi_hat_lambda − phi_lambda)² · 1{|phi_hat_lambda − phi_lambda| ≥ ε·√(n·I_lambda)} | 𝒯 ] ≤ C·I_lambda for all ε > 0
the score, bias, and drift integrands are measurable; the two score integrands are square-integrable

definitions.name: P_n
inputs: H_n: a probability law for U on [0, 1−η] with c₋·tᵏ ≤ H_n([0,t]) ≤ c₊·tᵏ for 0 < t ≤ t0
definitions.construction: U ~ H_n; A | U=u ~ Bernoulli(u); Y(a) | U ~ Bernoulli(mu_a(U)) with mu1 = g1

definitions.name: tau_hat_lambda
inputs: clipped cross-fit nuisances (mu1bd_lambda, mu0, e), threshold λ
definitions.construction: tau_hat_lambda = (1/n) Σ_i phi_lambda(O_i; mu1bd_lambda, mu0, e) — clipped AIPW score average

statements.statement: the clipped AIPW estimator at the optimal threshold attains E|tau_hat_lambda − ATE| ≲ rho_n
statements.justification: A clipped-AIPW estimator with a tuned clipping level balances bias and variance to reach the stated rate.
statements.gap: Chernozhukov et al. (2018) give only the strict-overlap root-n rate; the decaying-overlap rate is new.
statements.consumer: Practitioners running AIPW on observational data with limited overlap get an honest rate + tuning.

statements.statement: tail-localized minimax converse: inf over estimators T̂ of sup over P ∈ P_{κ,β} of E_P|T̂ − ATE(P)| ≳ rho_n
statements.justification: A least-favorable pair of laws in the class are statistically indistinguishable at the claimed rate, so no estimator beats it.
statements.gap: No matching converse exists in the literature for the overlap-decay regime.
statements.consumer: Establishes the rate is unimprovable, anchoring future estimator design on this class.

statements.justification: The two-point construction satisfies every defining property of the law class, so it is an admissible alternative for the converse.
statements.gap: Prior lower-bound sketches assume rather than verify class membership of the witness.
statements.consumer: Makes the lower bound rigorous, not contingent on an unverified construction.

sampling_model.draw: iid (O_1, …, O_n) with O_i = (X_i, A_i, Y_i)
sampling_model.cross_fitting: K folds; nuisances trained off-fold; ê_λ(X) ≥ λ and 1 − ê(X) ≥ η̄ on the evaluation fold

bibliography.citation: Imbens & Rubin (2015), Causal Inference for Statistics, Social, and Biomedical Sciences.
Rosenbaum & Rubin (1983), The central role of the propensity score, Biometrika.
Kennedy (2024), Semiparametric doubly robust targeted minimum loss estimation.
D'Amour, Ding, Feller, Lei & Sekhon (2021), Overlap in observational studies with high-dimensional covariates, J. Econometrics.
Audibert & Tsybakov (2007), Fast learning rates for plug-in classifiers, Ann. Statist.
Tsybakov (2009), Introduction to Nonparametric Estimation, Springer.
Chernozhukov et al. (2018), Double/debiased machine learning, Econometrics Journal.
van der Vaart (1998), Asymptotic Statistics, Cambridge University Press.

tldr: We pin down the minimax rate for estimating the ATE when propensity overlap decays in the tails, and exhibit a clipped-AIPW estimator that attains it together with a matching Le Cam lower bound.

project_justification.gap: Standard double/debiased ML rates for the ATE assume strict overlap; under tail overlap decay the existing root-n theory breaks and no sharp rate is known.
project_justification.niche: A minimax characterization indexed by the overlap-decay exponent, with a single estimator that adapts across the strict-overlap and decaying-overlap regimes.
project_justification.fill: We derive the achievable rate for clipped AIPW and a matching converse via a least-favorable two-point construction, closing the rate question on this class.

related_work: Chernozhukov et al. (2018) give root-n AIPW under strict overlap; D Amour et al. (2021) document overlap failure in high dimensions; Khan-Tamer (2010) study irregular identification under thin overlap. We sharpen the rate side these works leave open.
